title: he Faith of the Centurion part 1
scripture_text: ‘’When Jesus had finished saying all this to the people who were listening, he entered Capernaum. There a centurion’s servant, whom his master valued highly, was sick and about to die. The centurion heard of Jesus and sent some elders of the Jews to him, asking him to come and heal his servant. When they came to Jesus, they pleaded earnestly with him, “This man deserves to have you do this, because he loves our nation and has built our synagogue.” So Jesus went with them. He was not far from the house when the centurion sent friends to say to him: “Lord, don’t trouble yourself, for I do not deserve to have you come under my roof. That is why I did not even consider myself worthy to come to you. But say the word, and my servant will be healed. For I myself am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I tell this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and that one, ‘Come,’ and he comes. I say to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.” When Jesus heard this, he was amazed at him, and turning to the crowd following him, he said, “I tell you, I have not found such great faith even in Israel.” Then the men who had been sent, returned to the house and found the servant well.’’
devotional:
I wonder how we as a Christian community, understand the gospel? What is the gospel?

Let me ask, is it appropriate to share the message of the gospel without a gospel lifestyle? This leads to an other question. What is a gospel lifestyle? Or in what ways is our Christian faith counter-cultural to the world around us? If we had a ‘heart grasp’ of what Jesus taught in Luke 6, we could only conclude that his words were unconventional, counter-intuitive, counter-cultural, and radical.

Let me give you an example of some of the things he said.

‘Love your enemies.’ 6:27

‘Do good to those who hate you.’ 6:27.

‘Bless those who curse you.’ 6:28.

‘Pray for those who hurt you.’ 6:28.

‘Do not retaliate.’ 6:29.

‘Be generous and give to anyone.’ 6:30.

‘Don’t be afraid of loosing your possessions or look for payback.’ 6:30.

‘Love those who can’t love you back.’ 6:32-33.

‘Lend money without caring if you get it back or not.’ 6:34.

If you were to read on in the chapter you would conclude that Jesus is describing a lifestyle that is completely opposite and confrontative to how people live. In this chapter, Jesus is redefining how we should do life.

So, is He being unrealistic?

Is he expecting too much?

Is it even possible to live such a counter-cultural life?

The alternative is that we will help the lost to embrace their lostness.
prayer: Father help me to live a life that is so radical that people ask me what or who I am living for.
html:
he Faith of the Centurion part 1

Luke 7:1-10 T
‘’When Jesus had finished saying all this to the people who were listening, he entered Capernaum. There a centurion’s servant, whom his master valued highly, was sick and about to die. The centurion heard of Jesus and sent some elders of the Jews to him, asking him to come and heal his servant. When they came to Jesus, they pleaded earnestly with him, “This man deserves to have you do this, because he loves our nation and has built our synagogue.” So Jesus went with them. He was not far from the house when the centurion sent friends to say to him: “Lord, don’t trouble yourself, for I do not deserve to have you come under my roof. That is why I did not even consider myself worthy to come to you. But say the word, and my servant will be healed. For I myself am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I tell this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and that one, ‘Come,’ and he comes. I say to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.” When Jesus heard this, he was amazed at him, and turning to the crowd following him, he said, “I tell you, I have not found such great faith even in Israel.” Then the men who had been sent, returned to the house and found the servant well.’’

I wonder how we as a Christian community, understand the gospel? What is the gospel?

Let me ask, is it appropriate to share the message of the gospel without a gospel lifestyle? This leads to an other question. What is a gospel lifestyle? Or in what ways is our Christian faith counter-cultural to the world around us? If we had a ‘heart grasp’ of what Jesus taught in Luke 6, we could only conclude that his words were unconventional, counter-intuitive, counter-cultural, and radical.

Let me give you an example of some of the things he said.

‘Love your enemies.’ 6:27

‘Do good to those who hate you.’ 6:27.

‘Bless those who curse you.’ 6:28.

‘Pray for those who hurt you.’ 6:28.

‘Do not retaliate.’ 6:29.

‘Be generous and give to anyone.’ 6:30.

‘Don’t be afraid of loosing your possessions or look for payback.’ 6:30.

‘Love those who can’t love you back.’ 6:32-33.

‘Lend money without caring if you get it back or not.’ 6:34.

If you were to read on in the chapter you would conclude that Jesus is describing a lifestyle that is completely opposite and confrontative to how people live. In this chapter, Jesus is redefining how we should do life.

So, is He being unrealistic?

Is he expecting too much?

Is it even possible to live such a counter-cultural life?

The alternative is that we will help the lost to embrace their lostness.

Prayer: Father help me to live a life that is so radical that people ask me what or who I am living for.
Bible in a year reading plan: -Joshua 21:13-23:16
